seoDescription: Decision framework for selecting rental or ownership based on uptime targets, project duration, and lifecycle cost.

hero.heading: Equipment Rental vs Ownership
hero.body: Decision framework for selecting rental or ownership based on uptime targets, project duration, and lifecycle cost.

sections.title: Core Decision Factors
sections.body: The strongest commercial decisions compare cost, risk, and flexibility across the full operating lifecycle rather than headline pricing.

cards.title: Project Duration
cards.description: Short and variable projects often favor rental flexibility, while stable long-term loads may support ownership.

cards.title: Maintenance Responsibility
cards.description: Clarify who carries service, parts, and compliance obligations before finalizing commercial structure.

cards.title: Capital Allocation
cards.description: Balance capex constraints against long-term operating costs and redeployment potential.

sections.title: Procurement Pathways
sections.body: Many teams use staged pathways, including phased purchase, rental bridging, and lifecycle refresh planning.

cards.title: Rental-To-Ownership Transition
cards.description: Start with rental while demand stabilizes, then transition to ownership with validated load data.

cards.title: Refurbished Asset Strategy
cards.description: Consider quality used equipment where lead times or capex limits affect new asset procurement.

cards.title: Executive Business Case
cards.description: Build comparison models with downtime impact, response risk, and fuel assumptions included.
